name: X Persona Analysis
purpose: Understand a Twitter/X account the way you'd study a person, not score a personality test. Builds a deep, evidence-grounded profile of how they think (mental models, beliefs, reasoning, blind spots), how they speak (voice and style), what topics they engage with — and, crucially, how all of that has evolved over time. The two differentiators vs. existing tools (Wordware-style viral personality readouts, Big-Five scorers like Apply Magic Sauce, voice-cloners): depth of thinking and a real longitudinal trajectory. Not a shareable personality card; a structured profile that feeds a downstream agent workflow.
whenToUse: A manual Agent Run asking to deeply understand, profile, or track the evolution of an X/Twitter account — a founder, a competitor, a thinker, or yourself. This is a depth tool (consumes the full timeline, ~100k+ tokens), not a quick personality quiz. The caller supplies a normalized tweet corpus JSON (see corpus.schema.json); the skill does not fetch or scrape.
inputs: tweet corpus (normalized JSON: text + created_at per tweet)

dataContract.note: The skill does not fetch data. The caller supplies a tweet corpus (JSON array, see corpus.schema.json) and injects it as {{tweets}} along with {{handle}}; the runtime also provides {{now}}, {{history}}, {{utterance}}.

edgeCases: Thin corpus (few tweets or short window): lower confidence, say so in notes, and do not over-claim trajectory.
Mostly retweets/replies: separate amplified views from original takes; flag that voice signal is weak.
No timestamps available: fill dynamics with empty arrays and note that longitudinal analysis was skipped rather than inventing a trajectory.
Quote everything: every claim about thinking/voice/topics must cite a real tweet from the corpus, never a paraphrase invented to fit a pattern.
defaultCommand: Analyze the provided X/Twitter account and return a persona profile.
